title: Company Tax Registration & TIN in Bangladesh: Complete Legal Guide (2026)
metaTitle: Company Tax Registration & TIN Bangladesh (2026): Step-by-Step Legal Guide
metaDescription: How to register your company for tax in Bangladesh — TIN, VAT registration, corporate income tax, NBR compliance & penalties. Expert guide for businesses in Dhaka.
heroIntro: Every company operating in Bangladesh must register for tax with the National Board of Revenue (NBR). Getting tax registration right from the start — including TIN (Taxpayer Identification Number), VAT registration, and corporate income tax compliance — protects your business from penalties, ensures regulatory compliance, and enables you to operate legally across all commercial transactions in Bangladesh.
toc: Why Tax Registration Is Mandatory for Companies in Bangladesh
What is a TIN (Taxpayer Identification Number) in Bangladesh?
How to Get a Company TIN — Step by Step

How a Tax Lawyer Can Help Your Business

sections.h2: Why Tax Registration Is Mandatory for Companies in Bangladesh
sections.content:
Under the Income Tax Act 2023 and the Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act 2012, every registered company in Bangladesh is legally required to obtain tax registration before commencing business operations. Tax registration is required to:

  • Open a corporate bank account
  • Participate in government tenders and procurement
  • Execute commercial contracts above BDT 25,000
  • Import and export goods legally
  • Pay employees and deduct withholding tax (TDS)
  • Issue VAT invoices and claim input tax credits

Failure to register and comply with tax obligations exposes company directors to substantial penalties and in serious cases, criminal prosecution. A company lawyer in Dhaka can guide you through the entire registration process.

sections.h2: What is a TIN (Taxpayer Identification Number) in Bangladesh?
sections.content:
A TIN (Taxpayer Identification Number), now officially called the e-TIN in Bangladesh, is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by the National Board of Revenue (NBR) to every taxpayer — individuals and companies alike.

For companies, the e-TIN is the primary identifier used in all tax filings, corporate transactions, bank account operations, and government submissions. The e-TIN is obtained online through the NBR's etinbd.gov.bd portal.

Who must obtain a company TIN?

  • All limited companies registered under the Companies Act 1994
  • Partnerships, sole proprietorships, and joint ventures with taxable income
  • NGOs and charitable organisations with taxable activities
  • Foreign company branches and liaison offices

sections.h2: How to Get a Company TIN — Step by Step
sections.content:
Obtaining a company TIN in Bangladesh through the e-TIN system (etinbd.gov.bd):

  1. Gather required documents: Certificate of Incorporation (RJSC), Memorandum and Articles of Association, trade licence, bank account details (if already opened), and the authorised director's NID.
  2. Access the e-TIN portal: Go to etinbd.gov.bd and select 'Register' → choose 'Company' as taxpayer type.
  3. Fill in company information: Company name, RJSC registration number, registered address, nature of business, and authorised signatory details.
  4. Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of incorporation certificate and trade licence.
  5. Submit and receive TIN: After verification, the e-TIN certificate is issued digitally — typically within 1–3 working days.
  6. Visit tax circle (if required): For some registrations, a physical visit to the local tax circle under the jurisdiction of the company's registered address may be required to complete registration.

Note: The TIN alone does not establish your tax circle or tax zone. You must also register at the appropriate income tax circle office for annual return filing purposes.

sections.h2: VAT Registration for Companies in Bangladesh
sections.content:
In addition to TIN, most companies must register for VAT (Value Added Tax) with the NBR if their annual taxable turnover exceeds BDT 30 lakh (3 million). Registration is done through the NBR's Mushak Online system.

VAT Registration Process:

  1. Apply online at mushak.gov.bd
  2. Submit: TIN certificate, trade licence, company registration, bank certificate, and business premises details
  3. NBR issues a BIN (Business Identification Number) — the VAT equivalent of the TIN
  4. Once registered, you must: issue Mushak-6.3 VAT invoices for all sales, file monthly VAT returns (Mushak-9.1), and pay VAT to government treasury

Companies with turnover below BDT 30 lakh may be eligible for a turnover tax scheme instead of full VAT — consult a tax and corporate lawyer to determine the most appropriate scheme for your business.

Companies registered in Bangladesh pay corporate income tax on their net profits. Current rates (as of 2026):

  • Publicly listed companies: 22.5%
  • Non-listed companies (general): 27.5%
  • Banks, insurance, financial institutions: 37.5–40%
  • Tobacco companies: 45%
  • Mobile network operators: 45%

The corporate tax year in Bangladesh runs from July 1 to June 30. Companies must file their annual income tax return within the specified deadline — typically by November 30 of each year — along with audited financial statements certified by a registered auditor.

sections.h2: Tax Filing and Annual Return Requirements
sections.content:
Annual compliance obligations for companies in Bangladesh include:

  • Income Tax Return (ITR): Filed annually at the tax circle office with: audited accounts, computation of income, tax payment challan, and required schedules. Deadline: typically November 30.
  • Advance Tax Payments: Companies must pay advance tax in quarterly instalments — September, December, and March — based on estimated annual income. Shortfall attracts interest.
  • Withholding Tax (TDS) Returns: Monthly returns on deductions from salaries, contractor payments, and other services.
  • Transfer Pricing Documentation: Required for companies with international related-party transactions above specified thresholds.
  • RJSC Annual Return: Filed separately with the Registrar of Joint Stock Companies — includes audited accounts and board resolutions.

Non-compliance with tax registration and filing obligations in Bangladesh carries serious consequences:

  • Failure to register TIN: Penalty up to BDT 50,000 and prosecution under the Income Tax Act 2023
  • Late filing of income tax return: 2% interest per month on tax due, plus fixed penalty
  • VAT non-compliance: Penalty up to 3 times the VAT amount plus interest at 2% per month
  • Tax evasion (serious cases): Criminal prosecution, director liability, and imprisonment
  • Failure to deduct TDS: The company becomes liable for the undeducted amount plus penalty

Early compliance is far cheaper than penalties. If your company has missed filing deadlines, a corporate tax lawyer can negotiate voluntary disclosure and minimise penalties.

faqs.question: How long does it take to get a company TIN in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Through the online e-TIN system (etinbd.gov.bd), a company TIN can typically be obtained within 1–3 working days after submitting the required documents. If a physical visit to the tax circle is required, allow an additional 3–7 working days.

faqs.question: Is VAT registration mandatory for all companies in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: No. VAT registration is mandatory for companies with annual taxable turnover exceeding BDT 30 lakh. Companies below this threshold may be eligible for a simplified turnover tax scheme. A tax lawyer can advise on the appropriate registration category.

faqs.question: What is the corporate tax rate in Bangladesh for 2026?
faqs.answer: For non-listed private companies, the corporate income tax rate is 27.5%. Listed companies pay 22.5%. Banks and financial institutions pay 37.5–40%. Specific industries like tobacco and telecom have higher rates.

faqs.question: Can company directors be personally liable for company tax debts in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Yes. Under the Income Tax Act 2023, company directors can be held personally liable for tax dues in cases of fraud, wilful evasion, or failure to deduct and deposit withholding tax. Proper compliance from the start is essential.

faqs.question: What happens if a company misses its income tax return deadline in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: A late filing attracts interest at 2% per month on the tax due, plus a fixed penalty. Persistent non-filing can result in best judgment assessment by the tax authority and additional penalties. Filing late is always better than not filing at all.
